citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
On June 9, 2025, one pig underwent an alternate method for inducing a myocardial injury during a non survival surgery
that was not included in the IACUC approved protocol. A second pig had previously undergone the same unapproved
alternate method. At that time, a total of ten pigs had been used under the protocol.
The Principal Investigator (PI) did not submit a protocol amendment describing the change in surgical procedure to the
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) for review and approval prior to implementing the alternate
method. The Attending Veterinarian and the IACUC determined that the deviation did not result in any adverse impact on
animal welfare. No additional work was performed and the protocol was closed. The laboratory personnel were retrained
on IACUC policies and procedures, especially the requirements for protocol amendment and approval before any
changes are implemented.
Failure to submit proposed changes before implementation prevents the IACUC from reviewing modifications, verifying
that animal welfare standards are upheld, and ensuring compliance with the Animal Welfare Act.
The IACUC must have the opportunity to review, request modifications, and approve or withhold approval of all changes
to animal activities (protocols) before those changes are implemented.
Corrected prior to the time of inspection.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives.
n

narrative:
2.31(c)(7) 
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
On June 9, 2025, one pig underwent an alternate method for inducing a myocardial injury during a non survival surgery 
that was not included in the IACUC approved protocol. A second pig had previously undergone the same unapproved 
alternate method. At that time, a total of ten pigs had been used under the protocol. 
The Principal Investigator (PI) did not submit a protocol amendment describing the change in surgical procedure to the 
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC) for review and approval prior to implementing the alternate 
method. The Attending Veterinarian and the IACUC determined that the deviation did not result in any adverse impact on 
animal welfare. No additional work was performed and the protocol was closed. The laboratory personnel were retrained 
on IACUC policies and procedures, especially the requirements for protocol amendment and approval before any 
changes are implemented. 
Failure to submit proposed changes before implementation prevents the IACUC from reviewing modifications, verifying 
that animal welfare standards are upheld, and ensuring compliance with the Animal Welfare Act. 
The IACUC must have the opportunity to review, request modifications, and approve or withhold approval of all changes 
to animal activities (protocols) before those changes are implemented. 
Corrected prior to the time of inspection. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with facility representatives. 
 
 n
